revenue_by_merchant.sql:
SELECT m.merchant_name,
       SUM(t.amount) as total_revenue,
       COUNT(*) as txn_count
FROM fact_transactions t, dim_merchant m
WHERE t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id
AND t.transaction_date > '2024-01-01'
GROUP BY m.merchant_name
ORDER BY total_revenue
LIMIT 10;

issues.title: Missing filter for completed transactions
issues.problem: The query does not filter transactions by status 'COMPLETED' to calculate revenue.
issues.fix: Add AND t.status = 'COMPLETED' to the WHERE clause.

issues.title: Implicit join syntax
issues.problem: The query uses implicit join syntax which is less readable.
issues.fix: Replace the comma-separated tables with an explicit JOIN clause.

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic or purpose.
issues.fix: Add comments to explain the purpose of each clause.

review.corrected_sql: SELECT m.merchant_name, SUM(t.amount) as total_revenue, COUNT(*) as txn_count FROM fact_transactions t JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id WHERE t.transaction_date > '2024-01-01' AND t.status = 'COMPLETED' GROUP BY m.merchant_name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC LIMIT 10;
review.summary: The query has correctness issues with missing filters and uses implicit join syntax. It also lacks comments for readability.

customer_spend.sql:
SELECT c.customer_name, c.email, c.tier,
       SUM(t.amount) as lifetime_value,
       COUNT(t.transaction_id) as total_orders
FROM dim_customer c
LEFT JOIN fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id
WHERE t.status = 'COMPLETED'
GROUP BY c.customer_name, c.email
HAVING SUM(t.amount) > 1000
ORDER BY lifetime_value DESC;

issues.title: Incorrect SUM calculation
issues.problem: The SUM calculation is incorrect because it includes all transactions, not just those with status 'COMPLETED'.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as lifetime_value

issues.title: Missing filter for 'COMPLETED' status
issues.problem: The WHERE clause should be applied to the JOIN condition to ensure only completed transactions are considered.
issues.fix: LEFT JOIN fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id AND t.status = 'COMPLETED'

issues.title: Lack of comments on complex logic
issues.problem: The SQL lacks comments explaining the logic behind the SUM calculation and other complex parts.
issues.fix: -- Calculate the total amount of completed transactions as lifetime value

issues.title: Missing JOIN with dim_merchant
issues.problem: The query does not join with dim_merchant to show merchant names, which is against the business rules.
issues.fix: LEFT JOIN sigma_analytics.dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id

review.corrected_sql: SELECT c.customer_name, c.email, c.tier, SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as lifetime_value, COUNT(t.transaction_id) as total_orders FROM sigma_analytics.dim_customer c LEFT JOIN fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id AND t.status = 'COMPLETED' LEFT JOIN sigma_analytics.dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id GROUP BY c.customer_name, c.email HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) > 1000 ORDER BY lifetime_value DESC;
review.summary: The query had critical correctness issues with the SUM calculation and missing filters. It also lacked comments and did not join with dim_merchant as required.

daily_failure_rate.sql:
SELECT transaction_date,
       (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fact_transactions f2
        WHERE f2.status = 'FAILED'
        AND f2.transaction_date = t.transaction_date) as failed_count,
       COUNT(*) as total_count,
       failed_count / total_count * 100 as failure_rate
FROM fact_transactions t
GROUP BY transaction_date
ORDER BY transaction_date;

issues.title: Incorrect calculation of failure rate
issues.problem: The failure rate is being calculated incorrectly by dividing the failed count by the total count per day, which is not the correct way to calculate the failure rate.

issues.title: Correlated subquery
issues.line: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fact_transactions f2 WHERE f2.status = 'FAILED' AND f2.transaction_date = t.transaction_date
issues.problem: The correlated subquery can be inefficient as it runs for each row in the outer query.
issues.fix: Use a JOIN or a subquery with aggregation to avoid correlated subquery.

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic, which makes it harder to understand.
issues.fix: Add comments to explain the purpose of each part of the query.

issues.title: Ambiguous alias
issues.problem: The alias 't' is ambiguous and does not clearly indicate it represents 'fact_transactions'.
issues.fix: Use a more descriptive alias like 'ft' for 'fact_transactions'.

review.corrected_sql:
WITH daily_transactions AS (
  SELECT transaction_date, COUNT(*) AS total_count, SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'FAILED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS failed_count
  FROM fact_transactions
  GROUP BY transaction_date
)
SELECT transaction_date,
       failed_count,
       total_count,
       failed_count * 100.0 / total_count AS failure_rate
FROM daily_transactions
ORDER BY transaction_date;
review.summary: The query had a critical correctness issue with the failure rate calculation, a performance issue with the correlated subquery, and readability issues with lack of comments and ambiguous aliases.
